sense.definition: an instrument for writing or drawing with ink, usually made of plastic or metal
a tool with a slim piece of wood and a thin, colored part in the middle, that we use for writing or drawing
a type of pen that has a thick tip
a writing instrument filled with ink, typically alcohol-based, designed for writing on whiteboards, erasable surfaces commonly used in classrooms and offices
a writing tool filled with fast-drying, waterproof ink, typically used for marking on surfaces such as paper, cardboard, plastic, metal, and fabric
a writing instrument filled with pigmented paint or ink, typically used for creating permanent marks on various surfaces such as paper, metal, glass, plastic, wood, and ceramics
a special pen filled with ink that has unique properties, like glowing under UV light, used to mark items for security or proof of authenticity
a writing tool filled with liquid chalk, used for drawing or writing on surfaces like chalkboards or glass, and can be easily wiped off with a damp cloth
a white or colored stick similar to chalk that is used for drawing or writing
a type of chalk that comes in various hues, used for writing or drawing on chalkboards or other surfaces, commonly used in education, art, or decorative applications
a type of pen with a colored tip, used for marking words in a piece of writing, book, magazine, etc.

examples.x: She used a yellow highlighter to mark important passages in her textbook.
The editor used a pink highlighter to indicate revisions in the document.
Highlighters come in various colors to help categorize information while studying.
He borrowed a green highlighter from his colleague to emphasize key points in his presentation.
The student's notes were filled with highlights in blue and orange from different highlighter pens.

sense.definition: a highlighting tool filled with gel-based ink, used for marking and emphasizing text or passages in documents without smudging or bleeding through the paper

examples.x: During the study session, Sarah used a gel highlighter to mark important information in her textbook without worrying about ink smudges.
The office manager distributed gel highlighters to the team for annotating important sections in the project report without damaging the printed pages.
The student preferred using gel highlighters for color-coding notes in her planner, as the vibrant ink made it easy to organize tasks and deadlines.
Mark reached for a gel highlighter to mark key points in the meeting agenda, ensuring they were addressed during the discussion.
Emily used a gel highlighter to annotate her research articles, appreciating its smooth application and quick-drying ink for efficient studying.

sense.definition: a brand of highlighter pen with soft, translucent ink and a double-ended design, commonly favored by students, artists, and bullet journal enthusiasts
an object that consists of stiff hair or a man-made substitute attached to a handle, used for painting
a specialized writing instrument with a long handle and soft bristles, typically made from animal hair, used for creating elegant and expressive lettering in calligraphy and brush painting

examples.x: The calligrapher used a calligraphy brush to write beautiful Chinese characters on rice paper, showcasing the fluidity and grace of the brushstrokes.
During the Japanese tea ceremony, the host demonstrated the art of calligraphy by writing kanji characters with a calligraphy brush on a scroll.
Sarah practiced her brush lettering skills using a calligraphy brush and ink, striving to achieve the perfect balance of thick and thin strokes.
The artist used a calligraphy brush to paint delicate cherry blossoms on a canvas, capturing the essence of springtime in her artwork.
Emily experimented with different calligraphy brushes to create unique textures and effects in her mixed media paintings, enjoying the versatility of the brushes for artistic expression.

sense.definition: a small tool used for removing the marks of a pencil from a piece of paper

examples.x: He gently rubs the eraser over the words to erase them completely.
He lost his eraser and had to ask his friend to borrow one.
I use an eraser to correct my mistakes when writing.
She shares her eraser with her classmates during exams.
They keep a small eraser in their pencil case for quick corrections.

sense.definition: a tool that erases pencil marks with precision, featuring an eraser core encased in a pencil-like body

examples.x: Sarah used an eraser pencil to neatly remove mistakes from her drawings without smudging the surrounding areas.
During the math test, Mark corrected his answers using an eraser pencil to ensure his work was clear and legible.
The architect used an eraser pencil to refine the lines of her blueprint, making precise adjustments to the design.
Emily preferred using an eraser pencil for sketching outlines, as it allowed her to easily correct and refine her artwork.
The student sharpened her eraser pencil before the exam, knowing it would come in handy for fixing any errors on her paper.

sense.definition: a soft, pliable eraser made of a rubber compound, typically used for removing pencil marks from paper surfaces without damaging the paper

examples.x: The student used a gum eraser to carefully remove stray pencil marks from her exam paper before turning it in.
During the art class, the teacher recommended using a gum eraser to lighten graphite shading and create highlights in sketches.
Sarah relied on a gum eraser to clean up smudges and erase small mistakes in her artwork, ensuring a polished finish.
Mark borrowed a gum eraser from his classmate to fix errors in his notes, appreciating its gentle yet effective erasing action.
The stationery store stocked a variety of gum erasers, including larger sizes for heavy-duty erasing tasks and smaller ones for precise corrections.

sense.definition: a small tool made of rubber or a similar material used to remove pencil marks from paper

examples.x: She reached for the rubber eraser to correct the mistake in her drawing.
He carefully used the rubber eraser to erase the incorrect equation from the chalkboard.
The students shared a large rubber eraser to clean up their math worksheets.
The artist preferred a soft rubber eraser for delicate shading techniques in her sketches.
He kept a small rubber eraser in his pencil case for quick corrections during exams.

sense.definition: a rectangular tool made of felt or foam used to remove chalk markings from a blackboard

examples.x: The teacher used the blackboard eraser to clean the chalkboard after the lesson.
She wiped the equations off the blackboard with a swift motion of the blackboard eraser.
The students took turns using the blackboard eraser to clear the board before the next class.
He tapped the blackboard eraser against the chalk tray to remove excess dust.
The old blackboard eraser left streaks on the board, so they replaced it with a new one.

sense.definition: a quick-drying fluid typically used to cover mistakes made with pen, ink, or typewriter

examples.x: She used correction fluid to neatly cover the typo in her essay before submitting it.
The secretary carefully applied correction fluid to the errors on the typewritten document.
After making a mistake in his report, he reached for the correction fluid to fix it.
The teacher handed out correction fluid to the students to help them correct any errors on their assignments.
As the deadline approached, she worked diligently, occasionally applying correction fluid to her handwritten notes to make them more presentable.

sense.definition: a thin strip of white tape used to cover mistakes made when writing or typing

examples.x: Sarah used correction tape to fix the spelling error in her essay.
The office assistant reached for the correction tape to hide the typo in the report.
After making a mistake in his notes, Tom carefully applied correction tape to cover it up.
Emily lent her correction tape to her classmate who had made a mistake in their project.
The student's handwriting became neater after they started using correction tape to fix errors.

sense.definition: a handheld tool with a small blade inside, used for sharpening pencils

examples.x: She used a pencil sharpener to sharpen her dull pencil before the exam.
The artist preferred using a handheld pencil sharpener for sketching on location.
The office manager replaced the old pencil sharpener with a new, more efficient model.

sense.definition: a handheld or desktop device operated by hand to sharpen pencils by rotating them against a blade or abrasive surface

examples.x: In elementary school, we used a small, red manual pencil sharpener attached to the classroom wall.
My grandfather keeps a vintage manual pencil sharpener on his desk, reminding him of his school days.
When camping, we always bring along a portable manual pencil sharpener to keep our pencils ready for sketching in the wilderness.
The teacher handed out manual pencil sharpeners to each student, teaching them the traditional way to maintain their writing tools.
As a collector of office supplies, I have an array of antique manual pencil sharpeners displayed on my shelf, each with its own unique design.

sense.definition: a device that uses electricity to automatically sharpen pencils to a precise point

examples.x: The classroom was filled with the buzzing sound of the electric pencil sharpener as students lined up to sharpen their pencils before the test.
After years of using a manual sharpener, I finally invested in an electric pencil sharpener for my home office, and it's made my work much more efficient.
The sleek design of the electric pencil sharpener on my desk adds a modern touch to my workspace while keeping my pencils perfectly sharpened.
As an artist, I rely on my electric pencil sharpener to maintain the precise points needed for detailed sketches and drawings.
With its automatic stop feature, the electric pencil sharpener ensures that pencils are sharpened to the perfect point every time, without the risk of over-sharpening.

sense.definition: a specialized tool designed specifically for sharpening the tips of lead or graphite drawing instruments, such as mechanical pencils or drafting leads, to a fine point

examples.x: Artists often use a lead pointer sharpener to achieve precise points on their mechanical pencils for detailed sketches.
Drafters rely on lead pointer sharpeners to maintain sharp points on their drafting leads, ensuring accuracy in technical drawings.
The lead pointer sharpener in my art studio has interchangeable barrels to accommodate different lead diameters for various drawing techniques.
During exams, students who prefer mechanical pencils bring along a lead pointer sharpener to ensure their pencils are always sharp for writing.
Engineers working on intricate designs use a lead pointer sharpener to sharpen their drafting leads to razor-sharp points for precise measurements and drawings.

sense.definition: a tool used by individuals who are blind or visually impaired to manually create braille characters by embossing dots onto paper or other materials

examples.x: The student used a braille slate and stylus to take notes in class, pressing the stylus through the holes in the slate to create braille characters on paper.
The braille teacher demonstrated how to use a braille slate to her student, guiding their fingers as they embossed their first words in braille.
During the workshop, participants practiced using a braille slate to write their names and simple sentences, learning the basics of braille literacy.
The author, who was blind, used a braille slate to draft her manuscripts, creating tactile versions of her stories before transcribing them into print.
The museum exhibit featured a hands-on activity where visitors could use a braille slate to write their own messages in braille, promoting accessibility and inclusion for all visitors.
